symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral band-like squeezing headache, gradual onset over 3 days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral, pressing, band-like quality with gradual onset is the classic tension-type pattern.
Gradual build-up is atypical for the instantaneous thunderclap of SAH, but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Jaw clenching/locking, night-time bruxism, restricted mouth opening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nocturnal grinding with masseter pain and reduced opening indicates bruxism-related muscle strain.
Pericranial/masticatory muscle over-activity is part of the same stress-driven muscle-tension mechanism.
Jaw discomfort can overlap with GCA jaw claudication; here the pattern favours bruxism but GCA is not excludable remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe headache unresponsive to 48h of regular paracetamol and ibuprofen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A new severe headache resistant to simple analgesia raises the probability of a secondary cause and warrants in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No thunderclap onset, no focal neurology, no visual or speech change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of thunderclap and focal deficit lowers SAH probability but is not sufficient to exclude it remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no neck stiffness, no photophobia; nausea without vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent meningism and fever make meningitis unlikely; it remains a safety-net edge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic occupational stress, anticipatory work dread, rumination, isolation and self-doubt

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sustained occupational stressor is the driver of the muscle-tension mechanism and the adjustment reaction, but it must not be used to close off the secondary-headache work-up.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low mood and hopelessness about work; no suicidal ideation, able to stay safe

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Denial of self-harm ideation with preserved safety keeps this below a T5 crisis, but the screen itself is mandatory.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated blood pressure 148/92 with tachycardia (HR 95) — clinician-measured

investigation_details.expected_result: 148/92 mmHg, HR 95 (source in-person reading; no home monitor available in this encounter)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A hypertensive contribution to the headache is missed if the pressure is never measured.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An elevated reading alongside a severe headache warrants measurement and reassessment; sealed clinician measurement, not available to the AI Doctor remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical temporalis/masseter/occipital tenderness with trismus; cranial nerves intact — on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical pericranial/masticatory tenderness with intact cranial nerves supports a benign muscle-tension mechanism — but this examination can only be obtained in person, which is why the diagnosis cannot be confirmed via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these point strongly to a benign muscle-tension mechanism — but the cluster is only safe to act on once secondary causes are examined out in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A severe headache with a likely elevated blood pressure needs a same-day reading to assess a hypertensive contribution.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person cranial-nerve and neurological examination + temporal-artery palpation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to exclude focal pathology and giant cell arteritis before a primary-headache diagnosis is settled; cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ESR/CRP (if GCA suspected on in-person assessment)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for giant cell arteritis where the in-person examination raises suspicion.
Not to be ordered from telehealth and not indicated in the absence of thunderclap or focal red flags; would follow only from the in-person urgent assessment if red flags emerge.
